response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, versatile senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling and testing DNA — he authored test frameworks and runners (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), built CI/CD and device-orchestration infrastructure, created testing courses, and currently works on AI developer tooling. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and his resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, running one-on-ones, owning performance cycles, or leading organizational change. His mobile exposure is real but light (one iOS/Apple TV stint), and his leadership signals are community teaching and open-source founding rather than org leadership. He would be a credible senior/staff IC candidate on this team, but not a fit for the manager opening as scoped.
strengths: Deep, demonstrated developer-tools and test-infrastructure background (test runners, assertion libraries, CI servers, Selenium training, MCP tooling)
18+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Strong engineering-culture instincts: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, code katas, and knowledge-sharing workshops
concerns: No evidence of formal people management — no direct reports, performance management, or team-building experience listed
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or setting roadmaps beyond individual/founder-scale projects
Mobile-platform depth is limited to a single 2015-2016 iOS/Apple TV role, versus the JD's large mobile-ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Limited formal technical-leadership and roadmap ownership
